import json
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('articles/2026/01/03/articles.jsonl')
keywords=['Zelensky','economic adviser','Budanov','Pritzker','Larry Fink','Ukraine','Syria','Aleppo','Daesh','ISIL','Islamic State','AFCON','Algeria','DR Congo','Nigeria','Senegal','Sudan','Greenland','Denmark','Rubio','Trump','FIFA World Cup','British MPs','immigration enforcement','Minnesota','Minneapolis','Qatar','Board of Peace','peace council','Yemen','National Shield','STC','Iran','Lorestan','Kouhdasht','Azna','Lordegan','Grok','Oreshnik','wedding','Kyiv','embassy','Thailand','NIDA','Sabalenka','Brisbane','Nipah','Kerala','Somalia','Somaliland','Ruben Amorim','Manchester United','Marc Guehi','SDF','Kobani','Hasakah','Raqqa']
kl=[k.lower() for k in keywords]
seen=set(); count=0
for line in p.open(errors='ignore'):
    try:a=json.loads(line)
    except Exception: continue
    title=a.get('title') or ''
    content=a.get('content') or ''
    txt=(title+' '+content[:1000]).lower()
    hits=[keywords[i] for i,k in enumerate(kl) if k in txt]
    if hits:
        key=(title,a.get('source'),a.get('date_publish'))
        if key in seen: continue
        seen.add(key); count+=1
        print(f"{count:03d}. {title} | {a.get('source')} | {a.get('date_publish')} | hits={','.join(hits[:6])}")
        if count>=220: break
PY

### Anthony Joshua returns to UK after car crash in Nigeria that killed two of his close friends
www.skysports.com 2026-01-03 https://www.skysports.com/boxing/news/12183/13489932/anthony-joshua-returns-to-uk-after-car-crash-in-nigeria-that-killed-two-of-his-close-friends
Anthony Joshua has flown back to the UK after the car crash in Nigeria that killed two of his close friends and team members. Joshua sustained minor injuries in the fatal incident that took the lives of Sina Ghami and Latif 'Latz' Ayodele, with the former world heavyweight champion released from hospital on New Year's Eve. The funerals of Ghami and Ayodele will take place at a London mosque on Sunday. The driver of the car involved in the crash, Adeniyi Mobolaji Kayode, has been charged by Nigerian police with causing death by dangerous driving and driving without a licence, Ogun State Police Command confirmed on Friday. The case has been adjourned until January 20. The defendant was granted bail in the sum of 5,000,000 Naira (£2,578) and was remanded pending his bail conditions being met. A spokesperson for Joshua had confirmed his friends died in the accident, saying: "It is with the deepest and most profound sadness that we confirm, following a road traffic accident in Lagos Nigeria, the death of Sina Ghami and Kevin 'Latif' Ayodele. "Both were close friends and integral members of Anthony's team." Joshua, who has Nigerian heritage, was spending time in the country following his recent knockout victory over Jake Paul in Miami, the Brit's first fight since defeat to compatriot Daniel Dubois in September 2024.

Afcon 2025: Fixtures, results, tables and schedule in full from Africa Cup of Nations

The 2025 Africa Cup of Nations is into the knockout stages, as Morocco plays host of Afcon for the second time. Ivory Coast overturned a two-goal deficit to defeat Gabon 3–2 on Wednesday, after completing a dramatic comeback to finish top of Group F, edging Cameroon on goals scored. Mozambique advanced to the last 16 as one of the four best third-place finishers. Ivory Coast remain in Marrakech and will face Burkina Faso in the last 16 on Tuesday. Cameroon have a tough test against South Africa in Rabat on Sunday, while Mozambique meet Nigeria the following day in Fez. Other match-ups include 2021 champion Senegal against Sudan in Tangier on Saturday, followed by Tunisia versus Mali in Casablanca. Hosts Morocco will play Tanzania in Rabat on Sunday, seven-time champion Egypt face Benin in Agadir on Monday, and Algeria meet Congo in Rabat on Tuesday. Morocco stepped up to host after Guinea was stripped of staging rights over concerns about infrastructure and facilities in the country.
